premise: My brother-in-law convinced my terminally ill husband to change his $250,000 life insurance beneficiary from me to him, claiming I would just remarry and waste it. After my husband died, I sued for undue influence, won back every penny plus $50,000 in legal fees, and used the money to open a competing auto repair shop that bankrupted his garage within two years.

thumb.label: Thumbnail + metadata
